name: Event Labor Projection — Weekly Summary (pivot dump)
subject: Weekly count of events on the Comeketo calendar, Week 12 (mid-March 2026) through Week 31 (late July 2026). Source is a sparse pivot-table export — it shows event counts per week and a partial list of events named by host/couple, plus a parallel staff-assignment count pivot that appears misaligned. Best read as a demand-side calendar snapshot rather than a full labor requirement projection.
file_shape_note: The CSV has two side-by-side pivots that don't perfectly align. Left pivot: week → event count, with some weeks showing named events. Right pivot: another week index against staff-name counts, but the week numbers don't line up with the left pivot and a value of 52 under 'COUNTA of CATERING STAFF NAMES' for Week 17 is implausible for one week (matches the ~55 total roster). Right pivot is probably a separate totals breakdown, not week-matched. Treat event counts as authoritative; treat staff counts as advisory and confirm with Ops.

event_counts_by_week.note: Date range repeats Week 13 — likely a source-sheet error.
This is the current week (2026-04-22 falls inside).
Date range duplicates Week 20 — source-sheet error.
Date range duplicates Week 22 — source-sheet error.
Date range duplicates Week 24 — source-sheet error.
Peak week of visible calendar — 7 events.
Week 27 not shown — either no events or data not entered yet.
Date range duplicates Week 30 — source-sheet error.

totals.time_horizon: ~Week 12 (mid-March 2026) through Week 31 (late July 2026) — roughly 4.5 months forward from ingestion date.
totals.wedding_season_ramp: Week 19 (early May) → Week 26 (late June) is the dense band. 23 of 38 events (60%) fall in that 8-week window.

data_quality_issues: Several weeks have duplicate date ranges (Weeks 13/14, 20/21, 22/23, 24/25, 30/31) suggesting the source pivot was built from event dates that straddle week boundaries or the week-number column was entered inconsistently.
Week 16 missing between Week 15 and Week 17.
Week 27 missing between Week 26 and Week 28.

Right-pivot staff counts (3 bartenders, 0 extra staff grand total) are implausibly low — likely staff assignments aren't being entered into the source sheet for most events, or the pivot is missing a dimension. This sheet is NOT a usable labor-projection tool at current data quality.

Named events (Jessy, Alexandra Bebko, Donna Winnery, etc.) only appear for Weeks 14 and 25 — other weeks have counts but no lead/couple names. Whether the source sheet contains the names elsewhere or they're genuinely missing needs confirmation.

feeds_into: knowledge/staff_labor_roster.json — to answer 'can we cover Week 25 with 7 events from the current roster?' we need this demand-side calendar joined with roster capacity.
knowledge/venue_partners.json — if each event names a venue, we can check which venues cluster during peak weeks.
A future 'summer staffing' project — Week 25's 7-event peak is the single biggest stress test of the labor pool. Deserves its own project record if no plan exists.
operational_hooks.gap_to_close: This sheet does not yet function as a labor projection — only as an event calendar. To get a real projection, each event needs a planned staff count (captain + servers + bartenders + extras) and those need to roll up vs roster availability. Flag this to Jake as a tooling/process improvement.

notes: Treat this file as a week-by-week demand signal, not as staffing-side ground truth.

Week 25 (June 16-22) has 7 named events. Those lead names are candidates for event records — but event records require a venue, owner, quote, and date, which this file alone doesn't provide. Deferring to downstream files (CRM export, kitchen schedule, etc.).

sources.diff: Indexed Event Labor Projection Weekly Summary CSV — 38 events Week 12-31 (~mid-March through late July 2026), peak at Week 25 (7 events). 8 named leads/couples extracted for cross-reference. Multiple date-range duplication errors flagged. File is currently a demand calendar, not a labor projection — gap documented.
